title: Cardiology Progress Note — Visit 2024-10-20
text:
Patient Name: Robert Miller
DOB: 10/24/1966
MRN: 294-819-301
SSN: 000-12-3456
Phone: (555) 819-2049
Physician: Dr. Sarah Jenkins

SUBJECTIVE: Robert Miller is a 58-year-old male with a history of CAD, post-CABG in 2021, presents for evaluation of intermittent exertional chest tightness. The tightness occurs after walking 2 blocks, radiating to the left shoulder, and is relieved within 5 minutes of rest. Denies syncope, palpitations, or orthopnea.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 72 (regular), BP 138/84, RR 16, SpO2 97% on RA. General: Alert and oriented, in no acute distress. Cardiovascular: S1, S2 normal, soft S4 gallop heard at the apex, no murmurs. Pulses: 2+ symmetrically in upper extremities, 1+ dorsalis pedis bilaterally. Extremities: Mild 1+ bilateral ankle pitting edema.

ASSESSMENT: Exertional angina pectoris in a patient with established CAD and history of bypass surgery.

PLAN: Continue metoprolol succinate 50mg daily and atorvastatin 80mg daily. Add isosorbide mononitrate 30mg daily for anginal prevention. Schedule a nuclear myocardial perfusion stress test to evaluate for reversible ischemia. Dr. Sarah Jenkins will review at next return to clinic in 4 weeks.

title: Endocrinology Consult — Visit 2024-11-12
text:
Patient Name: Linda Davis
DOB: 03/12/1962
MRN: 849-204-184
SSN: 000-34-5678
Phone: (555) 304-9182
Physician: Dr. Edward Harris

SUBJECTIVE: Linda Davis is a 62-year-old female presents for management of type 2 diabetes mellitus and chronic kidney disease. Reports good adherence to her insulin regimen but has experienced occasional nocturnal hypoglycemia. Complains of mild bilateral foot numbness.

OBJECTIVE: BP 130/76, HR 68, RR 14. Labs: HbA1c is elevated at 7.9% (previous 8.4%). Serum creatinine is 1.45 mg/dL, with an eGFR of 52 mL/min/1.73m², consistent with Stage 3 CKD. Urinalysis shows microalbuminuria.

ASSESSMENT:
1. Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic nephropathy, currently suboptimally controlled.
2. Chronic kidney disease, Stage 3a.
3. Diabetic peripheral neuropathy.

PLAN: Decrease glargine insulin from 30 units to 26 units at bedtime to prevent hypoglycemia. Continue metformin 500mg BID. Initiate Jardiance (empagliflozin) 10mg daily for renal protection and glycemic control. Schedule follow-up labs (A1c, BMP) in 3 months. Dr. Edward Harris refers to podiatry for a comprehensive diabetic foot exam.

title: Pulmonology Consult — Visit 2024-06-27
text:
Patient Name: James Wilson
DOB: 07/04/1956
MRN: 712-409-582
SSN: 000-56-7890
Phone: (555) 928-3049
Physician: Dr. Robert Carter

SUBJECTIVE: James Wilson is a 68-year-old male with a 40 pack-year smoking history presents with worsening shortness of breath and an increase in thick, yellow sputum production over the last 4 days. He has a known history of severe COPD (GOLD Stage III).

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: Temp 99.0F, HR 92, BP 128/82, RR 22, SpO2 89% on room air (RA). General: Alert, speaking in clipped sentences, using accessory muscles of respiration. Respiratory: Barrel chest noted. Auscultation reveals diffuse expiratory wheezing and coarse rales throughout both lung fields. Cardiac: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs.

ASSESSMENT: Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), likely triggered by bronchitis.

PLAN: Initiate a 5-day course of prednisone 40mg daily and azithromycin 250mg daily. Cautious oxygen supplementation to keep SpO2 between 90-92%. Increase frequency of Albuterol/Ipratropium (DuoNeb) inhalations to every 4 hours PRN. Dr. Robert Carter instructed the patient to go to the Emergency Department if respiratory distress worsens.

title: Neurology Progress Note — Visit 2024-03-02
text:
Patient Name: Patricia Taylor
DOB: 12/18/1981
MRN: 601-839-402
SSN: 000-78-9012
Phone: (555) 739-1049
Physician: Dr. Laura Martinez

SUBJECTIVE: Patricia Taylor is a 42-year-old female presents with a history of intractable chronic migraine headaches with aura. She reports 12 headache days per month. The headaches are throbbing, unilateral, and accompanied by severe photophobia, phonophobia, and nausea.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals are normal. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII are intact. Sensation, motor strength (5/5), and deep tendon reflexes are normal and symmetrical. Mild photophobia noted during the examination.

ASSESSMENT: Intractable chronic migraine with aura, poorly responsive to over-the-counter analgesics.

PLAN: Initiate prophylactic therapy with topiramate 25mg daily, titrating up to 50mg twice daily over the next 4 weeks. For acute abortive treatment, prescribe sumatriptan 50mg orally, to be taken at the onset of aura or headache (maximum 2 doses per 24 hours). Dr. Laura Martinez recommended keeping a detailed headache diary to track triggers.

title: Orthopedic Consult — Visit 2024-05-12
text:
Patient Name: Michael Thomas
DOB: 09/05/1988
MRN: 482-901-384
SSN: 000-90-1234
Phone: (555) 283-9184
Physician: Dr. David Anderson

SUBJECTIVE: Michael Thomas is a 35-year-old male presents with right knee pain following a twisting injury during a soccer match 2 days ago. He reports hearing a pop, followed by immediate swelling and an inability to bear weight fully. Complains of the knee 'locking' and 'giving way'.

OBJECTIVE: Swelling and moderate joint effusion noted in the right knee. Range of motion is limited: flexion is restricted to 90 degrees due to pain. Tender to palpation along the medial joint line. Positive McMurray test and positive Lachman test.

ASSESSMENT: Acute right knee injury. Suspected tear of the medial meniscus and potential anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) tear.

PLAN: Obtain a right knee MRI to confirm ligamentous and meniscal pathology. Dr. David Anderson instructed on RICE protocol (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation). Provide crutches and a knee immobilizer for ambulation. Prescribe ibuprofen 600mg every 8 hours PRN for pain and inflammation.

title: Psychiatric Intake — Visit 2024-09-15
text:
Patient Name: Elizabeth Clark
DOB: 05/14/1996
MRN: 391-482-901
SSN: 000-12-9876
Phone: (555) 492-3048
Physician: Dr. Karen White

SUBJECTIVE: Elizabeth Clark is a 28-year-old female presents for psychiatric evaluation reporting persistent low mood, lack of energy, and generalized anxiety for the past 6 months. She reports difficulties concentrating at work and anhedonia. Denies suicidal ideation or auditory hallucinations.

OBJECTIVE: Mental Status Exam: Clean, casually dressed. Speech is slow and soft. Affect is flat and congruent with a depressed mood. Thought process is linear but restricted. PHQ-9 score is 18 (depressed mood) and GAD-7 score is 15 (severe anxiety).

ASSESSMENT: Moderate Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) and Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD).

PLAN: Initiate Lexapro (escitalopram) 10mg daily for depression and anxiety. Dr. Karen White refers the patient to cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) for weekly psychotherapy sessions. Educate on potential side effects, including temporary nausea and sleep disturbances. Schedule a follow-up visit in 2 weeks to assess tolerability.

title: Gastroenterology Consult — Visit 2024-08-20
text:
Patient Name: William Lewis
DOB: 11/02/1974
MRN: 502-839-104
SSN: 000-34-8765
Phone: (555) 829-1039
Physician: Dr. Christopher Lee

SUBJECTIVE: William Lewis is a 49-year-old female presents with a 3-month history of severe epigastric burning pain, which worsens after meals and when lying down. Reports frequent acid regurgitation and occasional dysphagia. Denies hematemesis, melena, or unexplained weight loss.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: stable. Abdomen is soft, symmetric, and non-distended, but with moderate tenderness to palpation in the epigastric region. No rebound tenderness, guarding, or organomegaly.

ASSESSMENT:
1. Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with possible esophagitis.
2. Suspected gastritis or peptic ulcer disease.

PLAN: Initiate Omeprazole 40mg daily, to be taken 30 minutes before the first meal. Dr. Christopher Lee advised dietary modifications: avoid caffeine, spicy foods, and eating within 3 hours of bedtime. Schedule an outpatient EGD (esophagogastroduodenoscopy) to evaluate the esophagus and gastric mucosa. Return to clinic in 4 weeks.

title: Pediatric Well-Check — Visit 2024-04-18
text:
Patient Name: Emily Robinson
DOB: 04/18/2020
MRN: 194-204-918
SSN: 000-56-6543
Phone: (555) 103-9482
Physician: Dr. Jessica Taylor

SUBJECTIVE: Emily Robinson is a 4-year-old female presents with her mother, Jennifer Robinson, for a routine well-child check. Mother reports the child is eating well, sleeping 10 hours a night, and meeting all developmental milestones, including speaking in full sentences and riding a tricycle.

OBJECTIVE: Weight: 16.5 kg (60th percentile), Height: 102 cm (55th percentile). General: Well-developed, active, and cooperative. HEENT: Tympanic membranes clear, throat without erythema. Heart: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, no masses.

ASSESSMENT: Healthy 4-year-old child with normal physical development and age-appropriate milestones.

PLAN: Administer scheduled immunizations: DTaP, IPV, MMR, and Varicella vaccines. Dr. Jessica Taylor provided guidance on safety, nutrition, and screen time limitations. Schedule the next routine well-child visit in 1 year.

title: Oncology Follow-Up — Visit 2024-05-02
text:
Patient Name: Susan Walker
DOB: 08/30/1969
MRN: 703-918-204
SSN: 000-78-4321
Phone: (555) 918-2039
Physician: Dr. Richard Green

SUBJECTIVE: Susan Walker is a 54-year-old female with a history of Stage II hormone receptor-positive breast cancer, status post-lumpectomy and radiation in 2022, presents for a routine surveillance follow-up. She complains of mild hot flashes and generalized fatigue but denies bone pain, cough, or local breast changes.

OBJECTIVE: Physical Exam: Lumpectomy scar on the left breast is well-healed, with no palpable masses or skin changes. No contralateral breast masses, axillary lymphadenopathy, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Lungs are clear and abdomen is soft and non-tender.

ASSESSMENT: Stage II breast cancer, status post-therapy, with no clinical evidence of local recurrence or systemic metastasis.

PLAN: Continue adjuvant endocrine therapy with Anastrozole 1mg daily for a total course of 5 years. Dr. Richard Green discussed managing hot flashes with lifestyle changes. Order a surveillance bilateral screening mammogram. Schedule follow-up clinic visit in 6 months.

title: Emergency Dept Discharge — Visit 2024-09-05
text:
Patient Name: Charles Hall
DOB: 02/15/1995
MRN: 894-102-394
SSN: 000-90-8765
Phone: (555) 203-9481
Physician: Dr. Thomas Baker

SUBJECTIVE: Charles Hall is a 29-year-old female presents to the Emergency Department complaining of high fever, chills, and severe right-sided flank pain for the last 24 hours. Reports dysuria, urinary frequency, and nausea.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: Temp 101.8F, HR 104, BP 112/68, RR 18, SpO2 98% on RA. General: Ill-appearing, shivering. Abdomen: Soft, but with severe right costovertebral angle (CVA) tenderness. Labs: Urinalysis shows pyuria, hematuria, and is positive for nitrites and leukocyte esterase, consistent with acute urinary tract infection.

ASSESSMENT: Acute uncomplicated pyelonephritis.

PLAN: Administer 1g of IV Rocephin (ceftriaxone) in the ED. Discharge the patient on oral Ciprofloxacin 500mg BID for 7 days. Dr. Thomas Baker instructed to take acetaminophen 650mg every 6 hours PRN for fever. Advised to follow up in 48 hours, and return immediately if vomiting or unable to tolerate oral fluids.
